title: FPM Garden
shortDescription: A retail journey in Tokyo inspired by Japanese gardens and Italian craftsmanship.
concept: FPM Garden translates the precision of Italian luggage into a quiet retail ritual. Movement becomes a sequence of thresholds, pauses, and carefully framed encounters with product.
spatialIdea: The store is arranged as a curated walk through garden-like rooms. Each zone invites interaction through changes in proportion, texture, and light, allowing the visitor to discover the collection rather than consume it at once.
materials: Brushed metal, mineral plaster, dark timber, ribbed glass, stone paths, and softened greenery create a dialogue between Japanese restraint and Italian tactility.

title: School Concept – From the Shore to the Depths
shortDescription: A primary school imagined as a gradual passage from shore to lighthouse.
concept: The school is conceived as an emotional map of the sea, turning learning into a spatial progression through curiosity, discovery, calm, and orientation.
spatialIdea: The journey moves from Shore to Waves, Reef, Depths, and Lighthouse. Each chapter shifts scale and mood, helping children understand transition through atmosphere and intuitive wayfinding.
materials: Pale timber, acoustic felt, translucent panels, blue-green accents, matte flooring, and soft mineral surfaces support a safe and immersive learning environment.

title: Apartment Interior – Milano
shortDescription: A calm, bright apartment for a young couple, shaped by openness and natural materiality.
concept: This Milan apartment is built around quiet generosity: daylight, uncluttered circulation, and a sense of domestic softness that does not lose precision.
spatialIdea: Living, dining, and kitchen areas open into one another with subtle thresholds. Storage is absorbed into the architecture, leaving the daily rituals of the couple at the center.
materials: Oak, linen, warm plaster, honed stone, brushed metal, and soft neutral textiles create an atmosphere of ease and lasting clarity.

title: Caffè Italia
shortDescription: Custom furniture inspired by the material memory of Milanese cafés from the 1970s and 1980s.
concept: Caffè Italia revisits the social elegance of Milanese café culture through furniture that feels familiar, cinematic, and exact.
spatialIdea: The collection is imagined as a set of intimate islands: tables, counters, and seating pieces that compose a scene of conversation and ritual.
materials: Red marble, dark oak, glass brick, lacquered details, chrome accents, and deep upholstery bring warmth and graphic strength to the proposal.

title: Private Villa
shortDescription: A minimal coastal villa shaped by horizontal volumes, shadow, and the movement of light.
concept: The villa is defined by calm horizontality. Its architecture frames the landscape without theatrical gestures, allowing light and shadow to become the main ornament.
spatialIdea: Strong linear volumes organize the plan into protected interior rooms and open terraces. The experience alternates between compression and release, privacy and horizon.
materials: Limestone, limewash, pale concrete, oak, linen, bronze metalwork, and low-reflective glass create a quiet coastal permanence.
